<div class="jp-OutputArea jp-Cell-outputArea">

<div class="jp-OutputArea-child">

    
    <div class="jp-OutputPrompt jp-OutputArea-prompt"></div>

>

</div><div class="jp-Cell jp-CodeCell jp-Notebook-cell   ">
<div class="jp-Cell-inputWrapper">
<div class="jp-InputArea jp-Cell-inputArea">
<div class="jp-InputPrompt jp-InputArea-prompt">In&nbsp;[50]:</div>
<div class="jp-CodeMirrorEditor jp-Editor jp-InputArea-editor" data-type="inline">
     <div class="CodeMirror cm-s-jupyter">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># [CHECKPOINT 8][5 points]</span>
<span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;</span>
<span class="sd">Finally, you should complete the code at the end to predict the price based on distance and stores.</span>

<span class="sd">Hint</span>
<span class="sd">----</span>
<span class="sd">At prediction, make sure you do the same feature normalization.</span>
<span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;</span>

<span class="c1"># distance = 2000, stores = 7</span>
<span class="n">X_test1</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">array</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">2000</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">7</span><span class="p">])</span>

<span class="c1"># distance = 3000, stores = 2</span>
<span class="n">X_test2</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">array</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">3000</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">])</span>


<span class="c1"># ======================= YOUR CODE HERE ==========================</span>

<span class="sd">&#39;&#39;&#39;# Predict price for distance of 3000 and 5000</span>
<span class="sd">predict1 = np.dot([1, (3000-mu)/sigma], theta)</span>
<span class="sd">print(&#39;For distance = 3000, we predict a price of {:.2f}\n&#39;.format(predict1))</span>

<span class="sd">predict2 = np.dot([1, (5000-mu)/sigma], theta)</span>
<span class="sd">print(&#39;For distance = 5000, we predict a price of {:.2f}\n&#39;.format(predict2))&#39;&#39;&#39;</span>

<span class="n">y_test1</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">dot</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_test1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_test1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">y_test2</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">dot</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_test2</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_test1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># =================================================================</span>

<span class="c1">#print(y_test1)</span>
<span class="c1">#print(y_test2)</span>

<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Predicted price of distance = 2000 and stores = 7 is </span><span class="si">{:.0f}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">y_test1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]))</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Predicted price of distance = 3000 and stores = 2 is </span><span class="si">{:.0f}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">y_test2</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]))</span>
</pre></div>

     </div>
</div>
</div>

</div><div class="jp-RenderedHTMLCommon jp-RenderedMarkdown jp-MarkdownOutput " data-mime-type="text/markdown">
<p><a id="section5"></a></p>
<h2 id="5-Additional-Questions">5 Additional Questions<a class="anchor-link" href="#5-Additional-Questions">&#182;</a></h2><p>In this part, you will solve two additional questions and paste your results in the corresponding place.</p>
<p><a id="section5.1"></a></p>
<h3 id="5.1-Gradient-Descent-without-Feature-Normalization">5.1 Gradient Descent without Feature Normalization<a class="anchor-link" href="#5.1-Gradient-Descent-without-Feature-Normalization">&#182;</a></h3><p>In 3.2, we applied feature normalization before using gradient descent to find the model parameters. Now try to use the feature directly without normalization and repeat the gradient descent process. Paste your codes, figures, and analysis below.</p>

</div>
</div><div class="jp-Cell jp-CodeCell jp-Notebook-cell   ">
<div class="jp-Cell-inputWrapper">
<div class="jp-InputArea jp-Cell-inputArea">
<div class="jp-InputPrompt jp-InputArea-prompt">In&nbsp;[59]:</div>
<div class="jp-CodeMirrorEditor jp-Editor jp-InputArea-editor" data-type="inline">
     <div class="CodeMirror cm-s-jupyter">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Load data</span>
<span class="c1"># We want to predict &#39;house price of unit area&#39;(7th column) based on &#39;distance to the nearest MRT station&#39;(3rd column) and &#39;number of convenience stores&#39;(4th column)</span>
<span class="c1"># Note that we use X instead of x to specify it is a matrix instead of a vector</span>
<span class="n">X</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">numbers</span><span class="p">[:,</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="mi">4</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">y</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">numbers</span><span class="p">[:,</span><span class="mi">6</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">m</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">size</span> <span class="c1"># the number of data examples</span>

<span class="c1"># print out some data points</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="si">{:&gt;8s}{:&gt;8s}{:&gt;10s}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;X[:,0]&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;X[:, 1]&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;y&#39;</span><span class="p">))</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;-&#39;</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="mi">26</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">i</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="nb">range</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">10</span><span class="p">):</span>
    <span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="si">{:8.2f}{:8.2f}{:10.2f}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">X</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">]))</span>

<span class="c1"># call featureNormalize on the loaded data</span>
<span class="n">X_norm</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">sigma</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">feature_normalization_multiple</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Computed mean:&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Computed standard deviation:&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># print out some data points</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="si">{:&gt;8s}{:&gt;8s}{:&gt;10s}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;X_norm[:,0]&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;X_norm[:, 1]&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;y&#39;</span><span class="p">))</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;-&#39;</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="mi">26</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">i</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="nb">range</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">10</span><span class="p">):</span>
    <span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="si">{:8.2f}{:8.2f}{:10.2f}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_norm</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">X_norm</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">]))</span>

<span class="c1"># Add intercept term to X</span>

<span class="c1">#NO NORMALIZATION</span>
<span class="n">X_no_norm</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">numbers</span><span class="p">[:,</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="mi">4</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">X_no_norm</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">concatenate</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ones</span><span class="p">((</span><span class="n">m</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">)),</span> <span class="n">X_no_norm</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">axis</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">X</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">concatenate</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ones</span><span class="p">((</span><span class="n">m</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">)),</span> <span class="n">X_norm</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">axis</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Testing for compute cost w/ multiple variables</span>

<span class="c1"># further testing of the cost function</span>
<span class="n">J_no_norm</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">compute_cost_multiple</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_no_norm</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">array</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">38</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="mi">7</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="mi">4</span><span class="p">]))</span>
<span class="n">J</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">compute_cost_multiple</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">array</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">38</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="mi">7</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="mi">4</span><span class="p">]))</span>

<span class="c1"># Choose some alpha value - change this</span>
<span class="n">alpha</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mf">0.05</span>
<span class="n">num_iters</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mi">200</span>

<span class="c1"># init theta and run gradient descent</span>
<span class="n">theta</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">zeros</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">3</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">J_history</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">gradient_descent_multiple</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">alpha</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">num_iters</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">theta_no_norm</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">zeros</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">3</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">theta_no_norm</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">J_history_no_norm</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">gradient_descent_multiple</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_no_norm</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">alpha</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">num_iters</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Plot the convergence graph</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">arange</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">J_history</span><span class="p">)),</span> <span class="n">J_history</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">lw</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">xlabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Number of iterations&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ylabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Cost J with Normalization&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Display the gradient descent&#39;s result</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;theta computed from gradient descent: </span><span class="si">{:s}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">)))</span>

<span class="c1"># [CHECKPOINT 8][5 points]</span>
<span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;</span>
<span class="sd">Finally, you should complete the code at the end to predict the price based on distance and stores.</span>

<span class="sd">Hint</span>
<span class="sd">----</span>
<span class="sd">At prediction, make sure you do the same feature normalization.</span>
<span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;</span>
<span class="c1"># distance = 2000, stores = 7</span>
<span class="n">X_test1</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">array</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">2000</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">7</span><span class="p">])</span>
<span class="c1"># distance = 3000, stores = 2</span>
<span class="n">X_test2</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">array</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">3000</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">])</span>
<span class="c1"># ======================= YOUR CODE HERE ==========================</span>
<span class="n">y_test1</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">dot</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_test1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_test1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">y_test2</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">dot</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_test2</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_test1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">-</span><span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="c1"># =================================================================</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Predicted price of distance = 2000 and stores = 7 is </span><span class="si">{:.0f}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">y_test1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]))</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Predicted price of distance = 3000 and stores = 2 is </span><span class="si">{:.0f}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">y_test2</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]))</span>
</pre></div>

     </div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

</div><div class="jp-RenderedHTMLCommon jp-RenderedMarkdown jp-MarkdownOutput " data-mime-type="text/markdown">
<p>What is your conclusion by comparing the two figures?</p>
<p>[CHECKPOINT 11][5 points]</p>
<p>======================= YOUR ANSWER HERE ==========================</p>
<ol>
<li>Non normalized data will not lead to cost optimization, in fact it leads to the opposite</li>
<li>Normalized data will likely not leave the bounds for python double scalars, while the non normalized data may as values get so large</li>
<li>Non normalized data will not give a correct answer</li>
</ol>
<p>......</p>
<p>====================================================================</p>

<div class="jp-Cell-inputWrapper"><div class="jp-InputPrompt jp-InputArea-prompt">
</div><div class="jp-RenderedHTMLCommon jp-RenderedMarkdown jp-MarkdownOutput " data-mime-type="text/markdown">
<p><a id="section4.2"></a></p>
<h3 id="4.2-Selecting-Learning-Rate">4.2 Selecting Learning Rate<a class="anchor-link" href="#4.2-Selecting-Learning-Rate">&#182;</a></h3><p>In 3.3, we talked about how to select the learning rate and we tried $\alpha = 0.1$. Now we will try different learning rate and see how the cost converges. We recommend trying values of the learning rate $\alpha$ on a log-scale, at multiplicative steps of about 3 times the previous value (i.e., 0.3, 0.1, 0.03, 0.01 and so on).</p>
<p>Paste 5 figures showing how cost_J changes with respect to num_iteration when $\alpha =$: 1) 0.03; 2) 0.1; 3) 0.3; 4) 1; 5) 3. Remember, You may also want to adjust the number of iterations you are running if that will help you see the overall trend in the curve.</p>
<p>[CHECKPOINT 12][5 points]</p>
<p>======================= YOUR FIGURES HERE ==========================</p>
<ol>
<li>You can save the plot to a file "xxx.png" </li>
<li>Copy "xxx.png" into the folder "./figure/"</li>
<li>Show the figure by checking above code about how to display a figure</li>
</ol>

<p>====================================================================</p>
<p>What is your conclusion from all the figures?</p>
<p>[CHECKPOINT 13][5 points]</p>
<p>======================= YOUR ANSWER HERE ==========================</p>
<ol>
<li><p>The learning rate increasing in logarithmic fashion would drastically increase both initial jumps in cost improvement, and had answer convergence happen faster.</p>
</li>
<li><p>This changed once the learning rate reached 3, where the cost would no longer converge but rather rapidly increase towards the end.</p>
</li>
<li><p>The learning rate of 1 appeared to converge the fastest.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p>......</p>
<p>====================================================================</p>

</div>
</div><div class="jp-Cell jp-CodeCell jp-Notebook-cell   ">
<div class="jp-Cell-inputWrapper">
<div class="jp-InputArea jp-Cell-inputArea">
<div class="jp-InputPrompt jp-InputArea-prompt">In&nbsp;[63]:</div>
<div class="jp-CodeMirrorEditor jp-Editor jp-InputArea-editor" data-type="inline">
     <div class="CodeMirror cm-s-jupyter">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Load data</span>
<span class="c1"># We want to predict &#39;house price of unit area&#39;(7th column) based on &#39;distance to the nearest MRT station&#39;(3rd column) and &#39;number of convenience stores&#39;(4th column)</span>
<span class="c1"># Note that we use X instead of x to specify it is a matrix instead of a vector</span>
<span class="n">X</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">numbers</span><span class="p">[:,</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="mi">4</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">y</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">numbers</span><span class="p">[:,</span><span class="mi">6</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">m</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">size</span> <span class="c1"># the number of data examples</span>

<span class="c1"># print out some data points</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="si">{:&gt;8s}{:&gt;8s}{:&gt;10s}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;X[:,0]&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;X[:, 1]&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;y&#39;</span><span class="p">))</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;-&#39;</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="mi">26</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">i</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="nb">range</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">10</span><span class="p">):</span>
    <span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="si">{:8.2f}{:8.2f}{:10.2f}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">X</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">]))</span>

<span class="c1"># call featureNormalize on the loaded data</span>
<span class="n">X_norm</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">sigma</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">feature_normalization_multiple</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Computed mean:&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">mu</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Computed standard deviation:&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">sigma</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># print out some data points</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="si">{:&gt;8s}{:&gt;8s}{:&gt;10s}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;X_norm[:,0]&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;X_norm[:, 1]&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;y&#39;</span><span class="p">))</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;-&#39;</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="mi">26</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">i</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="nb">range</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">10</span><span class="p">):</span>
    <span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="si">{:8.2f}{:8.2f}{:10.2f}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X_norm</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">X_norm</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">]))</span>

<span class="c1"># Add intercept term to X</span>
<span class="n">X</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">concatenate</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ones</span><span class="p">((</span><span class="n">m</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">)),</span> <span class="n">X_norm</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">axis</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;</span>
<span class="sd">Instructions</span>
<span class="sd">------------</span>
<span class="sd">We have provided you with the following starter code that runs</span>
<span class="sd">gradient descent with a particular learning rate (alpha). </span>

<span class="sd">Your task is to first make sure that your functions - `compute_cost`</span>
<span class="sd">and `gradient_descent` already work with  this starter code and</span>
<span class="sd">support multiple variables.</span>

<span class="sd">After that, try running gradient descent with different values of</span>
<span class="sd">alpha and see which one gives you the best result.</span>
<span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;</span>
<span class="c1"># Choose some alpha value - change this</span>
<span class="n">alpha</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mf">0.03</span>
<span class="n">num_iters</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mi">200</span>

<span class="c1"># init theta and run gradient descent</span>
<span class="n">theta</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">zeros</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">3</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">J_history</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">gradient_descent_multiple</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">alpha</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">num_iters</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Plot the convergence graph</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">arange</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">J_history</span><span class="p">)),</span> <span class="n">J_history</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">lw</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">xlabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Number of iterations&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ylabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Cost J Alpha 0.03&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Display the gradient descent&#39;s result</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;theta computed from gradient descent: </span><span class="si">{:s}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">)))</span>
</pre></div>

     </div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="jp-OutputArea jp-Cell-outputArea">

<div class="jp-Cell-inputWrapper">
<div class="jp-InputArea jp-Cell-inputArea">
<div class="jp-InputPrompt jp-InputArea-prompt">In&nbsp;[64]:</div>
<div class="jp-CodeMirrorEditor jp-Editor jp-InputArea-editor" data-type="inline">
     <div class="CodeMirror cm-s-jupyter">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Choose some alpha value - change this</span>
<span class="n">alpha</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mf">0.1</span>
<span class="n">num_iters</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mi">200</span>

<span class="c1"># init theta and run gradient descent</span>
<span class="n">theta</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">zeros</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">3</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">J_history</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">gradient_descent_multiple</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">alpha</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">num_iters</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Plot the convergence graph</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">arange</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">J_history</span><span class="p">)),</span> <span class="n">J_history</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">lw</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">xlabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Number of iterations&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ylabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Cost J alpha 0.1&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Display the gradient descent&#39;s result</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;theta computed from gradient descent: </span><span class="si">{:s}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">)))</span>
</pre></div>

</div><div class="jp-Cell jp-CodeCell jp-Notebook-cell   ">
<div class="jp-Cell-inputWrapper">
<div class="jp-InputArea jp-Cell-inputArea">
<div class="jp-InputPrompt jp-InputArea-prompt">In&nbsp;[65]:</div>
<div class="jp-CodeMirrorEditor jp-Editor jp-InputArea-editor" data-type="inline">
     <div class="CodeMirror cm-s-jupyter">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Choose some alpha value - change this</span>
<span class="n">alpha</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mf">0.3</span>
<span class="n">num_iters</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mi">200</span>

<span class="c1"># init theta and run gradient descent</span>
<span class="n">theta</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">zeros</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">3</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">J_history</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">gradient_descent_multiple</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">X</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">y</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">alpha</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">num_iters</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Plot the convergence graph</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">np</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">arange</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">J_history</span><span class="p">)),</span> <span class="n">J_history</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">lw</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">xlabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Number of iterations&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ylabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;Cost J alpha 0.3&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Display the gradient descent&#39;s result</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;theta computed from gradient descent: </span><span class="si">{:s}</span><span class="s1">&#39;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">theta</span><span class="p">)))</span>
</pre></div>

     </div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
